metadata.archetype_justification: Service owns a DynamoDB table with CRUD operations (POST to create books, GET to read all books) and manages entity lifecycle.
metadata.context: Serverless REST API with CDK infrastructure for book catalog management. Clean API surface the agent can use as a tool for product lookups.

findings.title: No Machine Identity Authentication
findings.description: The application uses Amazon Cognito for authentication on POST /books with implicit OAuth flow and USER_PASSWORD_AUTH only. GET /books requires no authentication. No client_credentials flow, API keys, or mTLS configured for machine-to-machine authentication.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication mechanism exists. Cognito is configured for human user authentication only.
findings.recommendation: Add a Cognito App Client with client_credentials grant type and configure a resource server with custom scopes (e.g., books/read, books/write). Alternatively, enable API Gateway API keys with a usage plan.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Add a Cognito App Client with client_credentials grant type and configure a resource server with custom scopes.
blocker_remediation.target_state: Agent callers authenticate via OAuth2 client_credentials flow or API key with principal attribution.

findings.title: No Identity Propagation or Delegation Mechanism
findings.description: No mechanism to distinguish between an agent acting under its own service identity vs. acting on behalf of a specific human user. No token exchange, on-behalf-of flow, or dual-identity pattern exists.
findings.gap: Cannot distinguish agent identity modes (self vs delegated).
findings.recommendation: Design token model with acting_as claim to distinguish self-service vs delegated calls when implementing machine identity.

compensating_controls: Use separate Cognito app clients for agent-as-self vs agent-on-behalf-of-user scenarios
Implement custom claims in JWT tokens to carry delegation context

findings.title: No Immutable Audit Logging
findings.description: API Gateway access logging is enabled at INFO level. No CloudTrail configuration, no immutable log storage (S3 with object lock), and no log integrity validation defined in IaC.
findings.gap: No immutable, tamper-evident audit trail. CloudWatch logs are mutable.
findings.recommendation: Add AWS::CloudTrail::Trail with EnableLogFileValidation and S3 bucket with object lock for log storage.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write operations requiring full audit attribution, but audit logging is still important for system maturity.
compensating_controls: Enable CloudTrail with log file validation
Configure CloudWatch Logs retention and export to S3 with object lock

findings.title: Cannot Suspend Agent Identity on Unauthenticated Endpoint
findings.description: GET /books requires no authentication. Without identity enforcement, there is no identity to suspend or revoke for read-only agent access. The only option is network-level blocking (WAF, IP rules) which is not configured.
findings.gap: Unauthenticated read endpoint means no agent identity lifecycle is possible.
findings.recommendation: Require authentication (API key or OAuth) on all agent-facing endpoints to enable identity-level suspension.

compensating_controls: Add API Gateway API keys to GET /books for per-agent key revocation
Configure WAF with IP-based blocking as emergency kill switch

findings.title: No Compensation or Rollback for Write Operations
findings.description: CreateBook performs a simple DynamoDB putItem with no compensation logic, undo endpoint, or saga pattern. No mechanism to reverse operations if a workflow fails mid-sequence.
findings.gap: No compensation or rollback mechanism for write operations.
findings.recommendation: Add a DELETE /books/{isbn} endpoint and consider soft-delete with status field for reversibility.

ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: Read-only agents do not execute write workflows, but compensation capability is relevant for system maturity.
compensating_controls: Read-only agents do not execute writes
DynamoDB point-in-time recovery could be enabled

findings.title: No Explicit Rate Limiting Configuration
findings.description: No explicit rate limiting or throttling configuration in IaC. No usage plans, per-method throttling, or WAF rate rules. Only default API Gateway account-level throttling (10,000 rps) applies.
findings.gap: No rate limiting to prevent runaway agent loops from overwhelming the service.
findings.recommendation: Add API Gateway usage plan with throttle settings and per-method throttling configuration.

compensating_controls: API Gateway default account-level throttling provides baseline protection
Lambda concurrency limits could be set as a safety valve

findings.title: No Machine-Readable API Specification
findings.description: No OpenAPI, AsyncAPI, GraphQL schema, or Smithy specification file exists. The API surface is defined only in the SAM template and source code.
findings.gap: No machine-readable API specification for automated agent tool generation.
findings.recommendation: Generate an OpenAPI 3.0 spec. SAM supports inline OpenAPI via the DefinitionBody property.

compensating_controls: SAM template provides basic path/method information
Manual tool definition based on source code

findings.title: No Structured Error Responses
findings.description: Error responses return HTTP 500 with empty body and empty headers. No error code, message, or retryable indicator.
findings.gap: Empty error response body provides zero diagnostic information for agents.
findings.recommendation: Implement standard error envelope: { error: { code, message, retryable } } in both handlers.

compensating_controls: Agent tool wrappers can implement retry based on HTTP status codes
CloudWatch logs for post-hoc diagnosis

findings.title: X-Ray Tracing Present but Structured Logging Absent
findings.description: X-Ray tracing enabled on Lambda and API Gateway. However, application logs use unstructured console.log without JSON formatting or correlation IDs.
findings.gap: Distributed tracing exists but structured logging with correlation IDs is absent.
findings.recommendation: Add @aws-lambda-powertools/logger for structured JSON logs with trace_id and correlation_id fields.

compensating_controls: X-Ray traces provide end-to-end request visibility
API Gateway access logs include request IDs

findings.title: Error Rate Alerting Present but Latency Alerting Absent
findings.description: CloudWatch alarms configured for Lambda function errors (threshold > 0). No latency-based alerting configured.
findings.gap: Latency degradation affecting agent responsiveness would go undetected.
findings.recommendation: Add CloudWatch alarms for API Gateway Latency and IntegrationLatency at p95/p99 thresholds.

compensating_controls: Error alarms catch catastrophic failures
X-Ray service map shows latency distribution

findings.title: No Pagination or Result Size Limiting
findings.description: GET /books performs unbounded DynamoDB scan with no pagination, filters, or sorting. All books returned in single response.
findings.gap: Unbounded result sets exhaust agent context windows as dataset grows.
findings.recommendation: Add limit and exclusiveStartKey query parameters for DynamoDB pagination.

compensating_controls: Current dataset likely small
Agent wrappers can truncate responses client-side

findings.title: No Input Validation or Schema Enforcement
findings.description: POST /books destructures request body without validation. No type checking, field presence validation, or format validation. Malformed input returns generic 500.
findings.gap: No input validation at API boundary. Malformed agent payloads fail silently.
findings.recommendation: Add request body validation (zod schema or API Gateway request validator) with structured error responses.

compensating_controls: DynamoDB rejects null/undefined for required attributes
Read-only agents do not submit POST requests

findings.title: No Temporal Metadata on Records
findings.description: DynamoDB items have no created_at, updated_at, or event_time fields. API responses include no freshness indicators.
findings.gap: Agents cannot determine when data was last updated or whether it is stale.
findings.recommendation: Add created_at and updated_at attributes. Include Last-Modified response headers.

compensating_controls: DynamoDB Streams could provide change timestamps if enabled
Book catalog data changes infrequently

findings.title: No Schema Versioning or Breaking Change Detection
findings.description: No API versioning (no /v1/ prefix, no Accept-Version header). No schema registry or breaking change detection in CI.
findings.gap: Agent tool bindings could break silently when the API changes.
findings.recommendation: Adopt URL-prefix versioning and add OpenAPI spec validation to CI pipeline.

compensating_controls: Staging E2E tests provide some regression detection
Small API surface limits change frequency

findings.title: No API Contract Testing in CI/CD
findings.description: Full CI/CD pipeline with unit and E2E tests. No API contract tests (Pact), OpenAPI validation, or breaking change detection tools.
findings.gap: Breaking API changes not caught automatically before affecting agents.
findings.recommendation: Add OpenAPI spec validation or consumer-driven contract tests to the build stage.

compensating_controls: E2E tests validate basic API behavior
Manual approval gate before production

findings.title: Test Coverage Lacks API Contract Validation
findings.description: Unit tests cover both handlers. E2E tests validate full API flow. No API contract tests or schema validation tests for agent consumption patterns.
findings.gap: No contract-level test coverage for agent-like consumption patterns.
findings.recommendation: Add API schema validation tests validating response structure and error formats.

compensating_controls: Existing unit and E2E tests catch major regressions
Pre-traffic hooks validate deployment correctness

findings.title: No Dedicated Sandbox for Agent Testing
findings.description: CI/CD pipeline deploys to staging environment. No dedicated sandbox for agent testing with production-equivalent data.
findings.gap: No dedicated agent testing sandbox environment.
findings.recommendation: Create dedicated sandbox stack with seeded test data for agent development.

compensating_controls: SAM local enables local testing
Staging can be repurposed for agent testing between deployments

evaluations.title: Documented API Interface
evaluations.reason: REST API documented in SAM template with clear endpoints (GET /books, POST /books)

evaluations.title: Idempotent Write Operations
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope — not emitted as finding since DynamoDB putItem provides natural idempotency on primary key

evaluations.title: Structured Response Format
evaluations.reason: JSON responses with Content-Type header — INFO finding recorded in MD but no remediation needed

evaluations.title: Asynchronous Operation Support
evaluations.reason: Extended question not triggered — no operations >30s, Lambda timeout is 5 seconds

evaluations.title: Event Emission for State Changes
evaluations.reason: INFO — no event emission but this is informational only

evaluations.title: Rate Limit Documentation and Headers
evaluations.reason: INFO — no rate limit headers but this is informational only

evaluations.title: Scoped Permissions (Least Privilege)
evaluations.reason: Authorization model supports scoped permissions via Cognito scopes and endpoint-level auth differentiation

evaluations.title: Action-Level Authorization
evaluations.reason: POST requires Cognito auth with scopes; GET is open — demonstrates action-level differentiation

evaluations.title: Credential Management
evaluations.reason: No application-managed secrets; IAM execution roles handle all AWS access

evaluations.title: Queryable Current State
evaluations.reason: GET /books returns current state from DynamoDB

evaluations.title: Concurrency Controls
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope — informational only

evaluations.title: Circuit Breakers and Resilience
evaluations.reason: Extended question not triggered — service only calls DynamoDB (managed service), no external service dependencies

evaluations.title: Blast Radius and Transaction Limits
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope — informational only

evaluations.title: Graceful Degradation Signaling
evaluations.reason: Extended question not triggered — service is P1 priority, not P0 or on critical path

evaluations.title: Draft/Pending State
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope — informational only

evaluations.title: Configurable Approval Gates
evaluations.reason: Evaluated as INFO under read-only scope — informational only

evaluations.title: Sensitive Data Classification
evaluations.reason: Stage A = No: book catalog data is not sensitive (isbn, title, year, author, publisher, rating, pages)

evaluations.title: Data Residency and Sovereignty
evaluations.reason: Book catalog data has no residency requirements — public product information

evaluations.title: PII Redaction in Logs
evaluations.reason: No PII in data pipeline — book catalog data only

evaluations.title: Data Quality Awareness
evaluations.reason: INFO — no data quality metrics but informational only

evaluations.title: Semantically Meaningful Field Names
evaluations.reason: INFO — all field names are clear and human-readable

evaluations.title: Data Catalog / Metadata Layer
evaluations.reason: INFO — no data catalog but informational only

evaluations.title: Business Outcome Metrics
evaluations.reason: INFO — no custom business metrics but informational only

evaluations.title: Infrastructure Governance
evaluations.reason: All infrastructure defined as IaC (SAM + CDK) with CI/CD pipeline and approval gates

evaluations.title: Rollback Capability
evaluations.reason: CodeDeploy with canary deployment and CloudWatch alarm-triggered automatic rollback

evaluations.title: Encryption at Rest
evaluations.reason: DynamoDB SSE enabled, S3 buckets use S3-managed encryption

items.summary: Resolve the machine identity blocker and address safety risks: implement machine auth, audit logging, rate limiting, and identity suspension capability.
Improve agent integration quality: add OpenAPI spec, structured errors, pagination, input validation, structured logging, and contract testing.
Enhance test coverage with API contract validation tests for agent consumption patterns.

recommended_actions.action: Implement machine identity authentication (OAuth2 client_credentials or API keys)
recommended_actions.rationale: Foundational blocker — all other identity and rate-limiting controls depend on having a machine identity mechanism.

recommended_actions.action: Add immutable audit logging with CloudTrail
recommended_actions.rationale: Audit trail is required for forensics and compliance when agents interact with the system.

recommended_actions.action: Configure API Gateway usage plans and rate limiting
recommended_actions.rationale: Prevents runaway agent loops from overwhelming the service at machine speed.

recommended_actions.action: Generate OpenAPI specification and add to CI validation
recommended_actions.rationale: Enables automated agent tool generation and breaking change detection.

recommended_actions.action: Add structured error responses and input validation
recommended_actions.rationale: Enables agents to self-correct on malformed requests and distinguish error types.
Prevents unbounded result sets from exhausting agent context windows.
